role_summary: Acts as a system power-path / load-switching board between PD input, battery input, and the main board.
Routes PD-board USB sideband and differential signals through board-to-board connectors.
Provides a muxed VSYS rail to the main board.

design_relevance: Configured to prioritize VBUS when available above threshold

firmware_implications: The BMS board appears not to expose a dedicated smart battery management IC to the MCU.
The main firmware likely does not control this board directly; it mainly receives VSYS indirectly and may optionally use GPIO6/GPIO7 via the battery toggle header.
ST_SDA and ST_SCL are routed through this board, so it is part of the physical path to the PD controller even if not logically active.
open_points: Connector pin population is only partially visible on the page render; cross-board mapping should confirm all unused/reserved pins.
The exact function of the battery-toggle header still needs system-level interpretation.
